common.defaultDescription: I help build complex web products, teach developers, and set up engineering processes.

directions.title: Directions
directions.subtitle: Pick what's closest to you — or just reach out directly.

mentoring.title: Mentoring
mentoring.description: Mentoring for beginner, junior, and middle frontend developers: fundamentals, practice, growth to senior.

development.title: Development
development.description: Complex SPAs, frontend architecture, CRM/API integrations, and technical audits for product teams.

education.title: Education
education.description: Program development and review, module authoring, lectures, and methodology for EdTech and universities.

approach.title: Approach
items: I design with room for the project to grow.
I minimize technical debt and unnecessary complexity.
I explain complex things simply — in code and in teaching.
I automate routine work: CI, quality gates, AI workflow.
I prefer stable solutions that are easy to maintain.

projectsPreview.title: Key projects
projectsPreview.subtitle: Open source and production case studies.

about.bio: Senior Frontend Engineer with 19+ years of commercial experience, 10+ of them with React and TypeScript. I specialize in complex web interfaces, high-load SPA/SSR applications, frontend system design, and building engineering processes. Worked with S7, VTB, Tele2, Sber (Edutoria), and EdTech companies.
about.educationText: MAI, Faculty of Radio Electronics of Aircraft.

courses.metaDescription: Recorded courses and educational programs on frontend development.
courses.heroSubtitle: Recorded programs and materials for developers
courses.aboutText: Author and expert of educational programs at Netology and other EdTech projects. Focus on practice, production approaches, and clear delivery of complex topics.
highlights: 150k+ students have taken programs I contributed to.
Frontend, React, TypeScript, Next.js — from basics to production.
Hands-on assignments, capstone projects, code review.

development.metaDescription: Development of complex web applications, frontend systems, and integrations.

ai.title: AI automation
items: LLM integration into products and internal tools.
RAG, agents, document and data processing pipelines.
Routine automation: reports, classification, content generation.

mobile.title: Mobile apps
items: React Native and PWA for B2B and internal products.
Companion apps, offline mode, backend sync.
App store publishing and release support.

chatbots.title: Chatbots
items: Bots for Telegram and web widgets for business needs.
Integration with CRM, APIs, knowledge bases, and support scenarios.

education.metaDescription: EdTech expertise: programs, review, lectures, methodology.
services: Review of materials and practical assignments.
Content for an interactive coding simulator.
experience: Netology — methodologist / expert / instructor / content producer (2018 — present).
Edutoria (Sber) — Subject Matter Expert for web development (May–October 2025).
RUDN University — course architect, «Digital Departments» program (~2023).
Yandex Practicum — mentor, learning team lead (2022).
GeekBrains — instructor for mass online courses (2017–2018).
numbers: 66 video lectures for RUDN, cohort of ~3,000 students.
Leadership group of ~24 experts on the Frontend course (Netology).
150k+ students have taken courses I contributed to.
Student retention consistently >80% against a 70% target.

mentoring.metaDescription: Mentoring for beginner, junior, and middle frontend developers: fundamentals, practice, growth to senior.
mentoring.heroSubtitle: For beginners, juniors, and mid-level devs: fundamentals, practice, growth
forWho: You're just starting out in frontend.
A junior who needs feedback and a learning structure.
A mid-level dev who wants to grow to senior and level up engineering skills.
You're taking courses and want personal support.
You're preparing for a junior or middle interview.
You're stuck on tasks and need a mentor nearby.
whatIGive: A roadmap from zero to your first offer or growth to senior.
Architecture, production practices, and code review for middle devs.
Interview prep for junior and middle roles.
Practice, habits, and confidence in development.
proof: RUDN: program and practicum for «Digital Departments».

items.question: Is it suitable for beginners?
items.answer: Yes, I work with beginners, juniors, and mid-level devs. I help build a foundation, grow to senior, and prepare for interviews.

items.question: How do sessions work?
items.answer: Zoom or Telegram, 60–90 minutes: reviewing code, homework, and your questions.

items.question: Can we go over a course or pet project?
items.answer: Yes — those are actually the most common. A work project works too, including under NDA.

items.question: Is there homework?
items.answer: By agreement: tasks between sessions to reinforce learning.

contacts.metaDescription: Get in touch with Vladimir Yazykov: Telegram, email, phone.
contacts.heroSubtitle: Message me on Telegram or pick a channel that works for you
contacts.faqText: I usually reply within 1–2 business days. Please mention the type of request, context, and a project link if you have one.

privacy.metaDescription: Personal data processing policy for this website.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.text1: By submitting a request through the form or email, you share your name and contact details so I can respond. This data is not shared with third parties and is used only to communicate about your request.
privacy.text2: For data deletion requests, email neizerth@mail.ru.

projects.metaDescription: Open source and production case studies by direction: Frontend, Backend, FullStack, team leadership, education.

arkham-divider.title: Arkham Divider
arkham-divider.tagline: Online card-divider builder with WASM, PDF generation, and FSD
arkham-divider.problem: Complex in-browser PDF and image generation for a board game.
arkham-divider.solution: Streaming PDF generation via vips-wasm, FSD architecture, i18n, CI/CD.

arkham-investigator.title: Arkham Investigator
arkham-investigator.tagline: React Native tracker with offline-first and a Rust module
arkham-investigator.problem: A mobile companion for a board game that needed to work offline.
arkham-investigator.solution: Offline-first, local multiplayer, native iOS/Rust modules.

amocrm.title: AmoCRM npm Library
amocrm.tagline: TypeScript library for the AmoCRM REST API
amocrm.problem: Typed integration with AmoCRM for Node.js projects.
amocrm.solution: Full API typing, TSDoc, open source.

cryptobazar.title: Cryptobazar
cryptobazar.tagline: A crypto market investment platform with real-time analytics
cryptobazar.problem: Needed a high-load investment platform with real-time crypto market analytics on top of a slow legacy backend.
cryptobazar.solution: Designed the frontend architecture on React/Next.js/Redux Toolkit, rewrote the Node.js backend with caching (60x faster), added WebSocket aggregation and Web3 authentication.

s7-airlines.title: S7 Airlines — internal systems
s7-airlines.tagline: 3 corporate web apps on a custom PHP MVC engine
s7-airlines.problem: Chaotic Excel file exchange and no digital dashboards for management decisions.
s7-airlines.solution: Aircraft interior rating dashboard, risk classification system with record locking, business card builder.

voice-social-network.title: Add Egg
voice-social-network.tagline: Clubhouse-style app — rooms and real-time audio comments
voice-social-network.problem: Needed a platform for communication via voice messages only.
voice-social-network.solution: Room and user management, auth and roles, real-time backend logic.

gymnastics-federation.title: International Aerial Gymnastics Federation
gymnastics-federation.tagline: Online judging for competitions during the pandemic
gymnastics-federation.problem: Competitions couldn't be held in person — needed live streaming and online scoring.
gymnastics-federation.solution: A platform streaming performances from webcams with real-time judge scoring.

yandex-practicum-league-alert.title: Yandex Practicum — League Alert
yandex-practicum-league-alert.tagline: Curated a team of 10 frontend developers on a student project
yandex-practicum-league-alert.problem: The student team needed real engineering processes, not an imitation of them.
yandex-practicum-league-alert.solution: Introduced a team-lead structure, peer review, and CI (GitLab/GitHub) for the League Alert nonprofit project.

edtech.title: EdTech — Netology
edtech.problem: Large-scale frontend programs for thousands of students.
edtech.solution: Course authorship, review, and leading a team of 24 experts.

rudn.title: RUDN University — «Digital Departments»
rudn.tagline: Course architect for a cohort of 3,000 students
rudn.problem: Students starting from scratch frequently missed classes and lost track of the course.
rudn.solution: Partially pre-filled coding assignments, built-in hints, a methodology resilient to missed classes.

ivr-vtb-tele2.title: IVR for VTB and Tele2
ivr-vtb-tele2.tagline: Interfaces for managing voice menu scenarios (AVR/IVR)
ivr-vtb-tele2.problem: A legacy AVR system needed frontend work for new clients.
ivr-vtb-tele2.solution: Built client-side logic for voice menu configuration and integrated it with the Java backend.

multichainindex.title: Multichainindex.com
multichainindex.tagline: A crypto project investment service built on Next.js
multichainindex.problem: Needed a crypto investment service without a separate backend for transactions.
multichainindex.solution: A React/Next.js app with Web3 wallet auth and direct frontend transactions.

crosspack.title: CrossPack
crosspack.tagline: An interactive landing-page store for meal plans
crosspack.problem: Needed a convenient service for choosing and ordering weekly/monthly meal plans.
crosspack.solution: Built an interactive order builder in React.

chrome-extension-parser.title: Google Chrome Extension
chrome-extension-parser.tagline: Offline DOM parser exporting to CSV/Excel/ZIP
chrome-extension-parser.problem: Needed to quickly collect data tables (e.g. employee cards) from pages without manual copying.
chrome-extension-parser.solution: From 1–2 sample elements, the script finds similar DOM nodes and generates a table with images.

beast-minimap.title: Interactive map for «Beast»
beast-minimap.tagline: A pet project that became an official publisher tool
beast-minimap.problem: Board game players lacked a convenient way to track markers on the map.
beast-minimap.solution: Built an interactive tool with multiple markers — the publisher included it in official add-ons.

fintorg.title: Fintorg
fintorg.problem: Manually collecting legal and litigation history data on companies took a lot of time.
fintorg.solution: A Node.js service for automatic authorization into «Kontur.Focus» plus FTP/XML tender parsing.

radio-components-tracker.title: Radio component tracking service
radio-components-tracker.tagline: A caching API proxy in front of a slow external API
radio-components-tracker.problem: The vendor's official API was slow and limited the number of connections.
radio-components-tracker.solution: A middleware Node.js server collected and cached data for instant repeat responses.

telegram-redirector.title: Telegram redirect service
telegram-redirector.tagline: A high-load redirect service to bypass blocks
telegram-redirector.problem: Needed alternative access to Telegram during blocks, monetized via ads.
telegram-redirector.solution: A minimal high-load service in Vanilla JS with an ad-management admin panel.

real-vision-studio.title: Real Vision Studio — e-commerce and 3D configurators
real-vision-studio.tagline: Online stores and visual editors on a custom PHP engine
real-vision-studio.problem: Standard engines of that era didn't support dynamic option editing or 3D previews.
real-vision-studio.solution: An async AJAX store, a visual JS product-option editor, and ActiveX integration for 3D furniture upholstery previews.

practical-psychology-university.title: University of Practical Psychology
practical-psychology-university.tagline: LMS, the «Psychologos» wiki encyclopedia, and the «Distance» module
practical-psychology-university.problem: An outdated Drupal-based LMS didn't scale and lacked student cross-review.
practical-psychology-university.solution: Rewrote the system, introduced cross-review, extended CKEditor, and shipped an OAuth2 REST API.

psychologi-online.title: «Psychologists Online»
psychologi-online.tagline: A forum with access gated by learning progress
psychologi-online.problem: Needed a platform where new topics unlock only after a mentor reviews the previous assignment.
psychologi-online.solution: A custom forum on Yii2 that auto-unlocks branches based on passed assessments.

moscow-property-department.title: Moscow Property Department
moscow-property-department.tagline: A survey and voting system for residents
moscow-property-department.problem: Needed to integrate surveys and voting into existing government infrastructure.
moscow-property-department.solution: Built a survey platform with results aggregation and analytics.

trenerskaya.title: Trenerskaya
trenerskaya.tagline: An online store for training programs
trenerskaya.problem: Needed a store with order management and payment acceptance.
trenerskaya.solution: Implemented order management and Robokassa payment integration.

video-lecture-schedule.title: Video lecture scheduling system
video-lecture-schedule.tagline: Tracking mandatory broadcasts for factory employees
video-lecture-schedule.problem: Needed to manage a schedule of mandatory broadcasts and restrict access by time window.
video-lecture-schedule.solution: A schedule grid, unique time-limited links for lecturers, and pre/post-broadcast attendance tracking.

bonjoin.title: Bonjoin
bonjoin.problem: An existing service-finder platform needed a simpler interface.
bonjoin.solution: Improved the provider catalog, search, and filtering.

antirabstvo.title: Antirabstvo
antirabstvo.problem: Needed a marketplace for matching clients with career consultants, with paid contact access.
antirabstvo.solution: A filterable catalog, a mutual-interest confirmation mechanism, and a personal dashboard.

own-web-studio.title: My own web studio
own-web-studio.tagline: A flexible team of 9–14 people: pet and Web3 products
own-web-studio.problem: Needed to build a full-cycle studio for high-load and Web3 products.
own-web-studio.solution: Assembled and led a flexible team of markup, frontend/backend developers, and QA/PM.

fiztekh-union.title: FizTech College / UniOn
fiztekh-union.tagline: In-person web development teaching for schoolchildren
fiztekh-union.problem: The web development program was weak and didn't prepare students for the real market.
fiztekh-union.solution: Proactively reworked the program around applied skills — some students went on into the profession.

geekbrains.title: GeekBrains
geekbrains.tagline: Mass online courses run under Mail.ru Group
geekbrains.problem: A mass beginner audience needed a resilient methodology under high stress and strict KPIs.
geekbrains.solution: Led cohorts of up to 10,000 students, keeping the rating above the platform's cutoff.

skolkovo.title: Skolkovo
skolkovo.problem: Teams of schoolchildren and students needed expert refinement of startup ideas before pitching.
skolkovo.solution: Mentored the group, assessed technical feasibility, and estimated MVP development cost.

tel-ran.title: Tel-Ran
tel-ran.tagline: Full-cycle bootcamp for expats in Germany
tel-ran.problem: Russian-speaking expats needed intensive retraining for the German job market.
tel-ran.solution: Ran an intensive 3 hours, 4 days a week — the whole group defended their capstone projects.

edutoria.title: Edutoria (Sber)
edutoria.tagline: Subject Matter Expert for a web development course
edutoria.problem: Needed expert validation of learning outcomes in a product-oriented EdTech team.
edutoria.solution: Wrote lessons and assignments, validated Learning Outcomes, worked Scrum with a methodologist and editor.

tutorplace.title: TutorPlace
tutorplace.tagline: Learning path architect for web development
tutorplace.problem: The platform needed structured programs to launch and scale.
tutorplace.solution: Designed 4 programs, from learning paths to students' practical outcomes.

innopolis.title: Innopolis University
innopolis.tagline: A «zero to production» Fullstack program on Node.js
innopolis.problem: Developers with basic training lacked hands-on production backend experience.
innopolis.solution: Built a course from Node.js/Express through Docker, testing, and CI/CD, with assessments.

ai-audio-transcriber.title: AI Audio Transcriber
ai-audio-transcriber.tagline: A desktop app for automatic audio transcription
ai-audio-transcriber.problem: Needed a single tool to automatically transcribe voice messages into text.
ai-audio-transcriber.solution: Built a desktop app on Electron with a Python backend for Whisper and GigaAM ASR model inference, set up audio ingestion via a Telegram bot.

resume.metaDescription: Resume by direction: Frontend, Backend, Fullstack, Team Lead, education and mentoring.
resume.heroSubtitle: Choose a direction — each has its own resume
resume.education: MAI, Faculty of Radio Electronics of Aircraft

frontend.title: Frontend Developer
frontend.tagline: React, TypeScript, Next.js — 10+ years in production, in web since 2007
frontend.summary: Frontend engineer and architect with 19+ years of commercial development and 10+ years with React/TypeScript. I design high-load SPA/SSR applications, optimize performance, and revive legacy systems. Worked with S7, VTB, Tele2, Sber (Edutoria), and EdTech platforms.

experience.companyLine: A crypto market investment platform with real-time analytics
experience.mission: Designed and grew the frontend architecture of a high-load investment platform over 6 years.
bullets: Designed the architecture on React/Next.js/TypeScript, separating server and UI state (Redux Toolkit) to simplify caching and module scaling.
Built a component structure on Atomic Design with CSS Modules and BEM, easing UI reuse and design-system growth; animations on Framer Motion.
Reduced interface latency by implementing streaming WebSocket data aggregation and caching.
Integrated Web3 authentication and transaction execution directly from the frontend.
Optimized performance for large datasets: list virtualization, memoization, code splitting.

experience.companyLine: Corporate systems and education platforms, external technical lead
experience.mission: Migrated legacy interfaces to React/Vue without stopping development; designed new modules.
bullets: Ran a gradual migration of legacy jQuery interfaces to React and Vue without stopping business processes.
Designed and shipped an LMS platform with video courses, time-limited content access, and learning analytics.
Introduced code review standards and regular architecture discussions within the team.

experience.companyLine: Pet projects in production for a board game
experience.mission: Designed and built two production apps from scratch on an FSD architecture for thousands of users.
bullets: Cut the initial bundle to 200 KB across 1500+ TS files via code splitting and lazy loading.
Implemented streaming PDF and image generation in the browser using WebAssembly (vips-wasm).
Integrated a Rust probability-calculation module and a native iOS module in the second app.

experience.companyLine: Online judging platform for competitions
experience.mission: Built the client side of a live-streaming and real-time scoring system during the pandemic.
bullets: Built a real-time scoring interface in Vue.js and React for the judging panel.
Ensured uninterrupted interface performance during simultaneous webcam streams from participants.

backend.title: Backend Developer
backend.tagline: Node.js, NestJS, PHP/Laravel — APIs, integrations, performance
backend.summary: 19+ years of commercial development, a significant part in backend systems and integrations: from custom PHP MVC engines to Node.js APIs. Sped up service response times by tens of times; designed internal systems for S7 Airlines.

experience.companyLine: Investor API Dashboard — a crypto investment service
experience.mission: Rewrote the investment platform’s Node.js backend, speeding up API response 60x.
bullets: Sped up API response 60x by replacing direct Google Sheets requests with pre-caching.
Designed aggregation and multi-layer caching for streaming WebSocket data.
Built supporting Node.js services for crypto portfolio data aggregation.

experience.companyLine: A government procurement monitoring service
experience.mission: Automated the collection and enrichment of legal data on companies from government tenders.
bullets: Implemented FTP/XML tender data parsing with storage by procurement number.
Built a Node.js service for automatic authorization into "Kontur.Focus" via reading emails on a dummy mailbox.
Removed the manual barrier to retrieving legal and litigation history by company registration number.

experience.companyLine: A major airline, internal corporate systems
experience.mission: Built three internal web applications from scratch on a custom PHP MVC engine.
bullets: Cut managers’ data-processing time by 3–4 hours a day by replacing Excel file exchange with a unified platform.
Implemented a record-locking and real-time collaboration system for multiple departments.
Designed an aircraft interior condition rating dashboard with a photo-based rating system.

fullstack-node.title: Fullstack Developer (Node.js)
fullstack-node.tagline: React + Node.js/NestJS — from architecture to production
fullstack-node.summary: Full-cycle development on React/Next.js + Node.js: architecture, database, deployment, monitoring. Experience with WebSocket real-time systems, Web3 integrations, and AI/ASR pipelines.

experience.companyLine: A crypto market investment platform
experience.mission: Owned the product end to end — from frontend architecture to backend performance.
bullets: Designed the frontend on React/Next.js and rewrote the Node.js backend, speeding up API response 60x.
Implemented real-time WebSocket analytics with multi-layer caching for streaming data.
Implemented Web3 authentication and transaction execution directly from the client app.

experience.companyLine: A desktop app for automatic audio transcription (pet project)
experience.mission: Built an end-to-end voice-message transcription pipeline using AI models.
bullets: Built a desktop app on Electron (React/TypeScript) with a Python backend for ASR model inference.
Integrated Whisper and GigaAM models for automatic speech recognition.
Set up an automated audio-ingestion pipeline via the Telegram Bot API.

experience.company: My own web studio / independent practice
experience.companyLine: A flexible full-cycle team, 9–14 people
experience.mission: Built high-load pet and Web3 products with a flexible fullstack team.
bullets: Built distributed system architecture and Web3 integration for investment platforms.
Implemented middleware caching and backend optimization for high load in B2B services.
Built Chrome extensions and parsers as internal studio tools.

experience.companyLine: A voice-chat platform (Clubhouse-style)
experience.mission: Built the backend and technical architecture of a voice social network from scratch.
bullets: Implemented audio comment recording and sharing with real-time backend logic on Laravel.
Designed room, user, authentication, and role management systems.
Provided technical expertise and coordinated a team of 2 developers and a manager.

fullstack-php.title: Fullstack Developer (PHP)
fullstack-php.tagline: Laravel, Yii2 — from MVP to production for 15+ years
fullstack-php.summary: PHP was one of the first stacks in my career and is still an active tool today. Full cycle: from custom MVC engines to Laravel/Yii2 production, including stints as a startup CTO and external technical lead.

experience.companyLine: LMS platform and corporate systems on PHP
experience.mission: Designed and shipped an LMS platform on Laravel/Yii2.
bullets: Designed an LMS platform with video courses, time-limited content access, and learning analytics.
Migrated legacy jQuery interfaces to React/Vue without stopping business processes.
Introduced REST API, OAuth2, and code review standards for the team.

experience.companyLine: A startup in online education and psychology
experience.mission: Built the product from scratch solo — from backend architecture to business co-ownership.
bullets: Built a custom Yii/Yii2 forum that auto-unlocks branches based on passed assessments.
Designed the product’s entire backend architecture as the company’s sole developer.
Implemented a moderation system and student-mentor interaction workflow.

experience.companyLine: An educational platform with an LMS and wiki encyclopedia
experience.mission: Modernized an outdated LMS and wiki platform for thousands of users.
bullets: Rewrote the LMS off Drupal, introducing cross-review (three-peer plus self-review).
Built an OAuth2 REST API for the "Distance" group coaching module on a Yii backend.
Extended CKEditor with Markdown/HTML/Wiki parsers and an article categorization system.

experience.companyLine: A career consultant marketplace
experience.mission: Built a career consultant marketplace on Laravel from scratch.
bullets: Implemented dynamic catalog filtering by specialty, experience, and price.
Designed a mutual-interest confirmation mechanism with paid contact access.
Built a personal dashboard for specialists and clients with request handling.

team-lead.title: Team Lead / Technical Manager
team-lead.tagline: Teams of up to 12 — process, architecture, mentorship
team-lead.summary: 5+ years of management experience on top of 19+ years of engineering practice: from startup CTO to external technical lead and student-team curator. Led distributed teams of up to 12 engineers, built processes (Git Flow, peer review, CI/CD).

experience.companyLine: S7, International Aerial Gymnastics Federation, and others
experience.mission: Led distributed teams of up to 12 engineers building corporate systems.
bullets: Assembled and led a team of 9–14 (frontend, backend, QA/PM) for the International Aerial Gymnastics Federation — an online judging platform with live streaming.
Introduced Git Flow, peer review, and CI/CD, unifying processes across different teams.
Performed technical assessment of projects and supported teams through implementation.

experience.companyLine: External technical lead, corporate systems
experience.mission: Owned architectural decisions and technical project assessment together with clients.
bullets: Made architectural decisions and led technical project assessment together with analysts and clients.
Introduced code review standards and task decomposition, mentored the team’s developers.

experience.companyLine: A flexible full-cycle web studio, 9–14 people
experience.mission: Built and led a flexible full-cycle development team.
bullets: Led a team of 9–14 specialists (markup, frontend, backend, QA/PM) across multiple projects.
Introduced technical audits and architecture reviews as a regular studio practice.
Owned hiring, task allocation, and technical expertise across all studio projects.

experience.companyLine: League Alert, a nonprofit fullstack student project
experience.mission: Ran a student team as a real engineering project with production processes.
bullets: Introduced a team-lead structure and peer review for a team of 10 frontend developers.
Set up real CI (GitLab/GitHub) and task tracking in Notion instead of simulated process.
Coordinated result demos to the client over the project’s 9-month run.

mentoring-education.title: Education & Mentoring
mentoring-education.tagline: 15+ years in IT education — from schoolchildren to production developers
mentoring-education.summary: I’ve been teaching and designing educational programs since 2009: trained 10,000+ developers (GeekBrains, Netology, RUDN), mentor Middle/Middle+ engineers one-on-one, and teach professional-development courses at OTUS. 4.8–4.9 rating from 4,500+ reviews, retention >80%.

experience.companyLine: A federal EdTech platform
experience.mission: Run the full cycle of educational programs — from methodology to recording courses.
bullets: Recorded about 10 studio courses on JS, React, and Node.js as lead instructor.
Developed 24+ homework assignments for the «Basic JavaScript in the Browser» course, some still used since 2019.
Member of a 24-expert leadership group setting the program's quality benchmarks.

experience.companyLine: A mass online platform (Mail.ru Group)
experience.mission: Led cohorts of up to 10,000 students, keeping the rating above the platform's cutoff.
bullets: Held a 4.8–4.9 rating from 4,500+ reviews against the platform's 4.6 cutoff.
Handled an overshoot enrollment of 10,000 students instead of the planned 2,000.
Launched open morning sessions for beginners that seeded my YouTube channel.

experience.companyLine: «Digital Departments» continuing-education program
experience.mission: Designed a course resilient to missed classes for a cohort of 3,000 students, from scratch.
bullets: Recorded 66 video lectures and built partially pre-filled coding assignments for beginners.
Kept an active core of ~700 out of 3,000 students under a 21–23 hour/week workload.
Designed a methodology that let students continue learning after missed classes.
